body:
## Summary
- Add `base_branch` and `base_pr` inputs for implementation runs.
- Resolve implementation bases with validation, same-repo open PR head lookup, and clear ambiguous-input failures.
- Forward stacked base inputs through implement and orchestrator dispatch paths.
- Document stacked PR behavior and add focused regression coverage.

## Verification
- `npm test`
- `npm run build && node --test dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js dist/__tests__/implementation-base.test.js dist/__tests__/dispatch-agent-implement-cli.test.js dist/__tests__/orchestrate-handoff-cli.test.js`

Closes #30

comments.body:
Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `implement` -> `review` (round 3/10).

agent planner selected review: Implementation succeeded and produced PR #56 for issue #30; no review has run yet, so an automated review is the useful next step.

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Orchestrator handoff keeps `requested_by` in shared inputs and forwards only structured `base_branch`/`base_pr` inputs to `agent-implement.yml`; no broader delegated route is introduced. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is limited to stacked implementation bases: implement/orchestrator workflow inputs, dispatch plumbing, one resolver helper/CLI, focused tests, and matching docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | `resolveImplementationBase()` reuses existing `fetchPrMeta()` and workflow dispatch helpers instead of embedding GitHub API logic in shell. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates `.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md`, `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md`, and `.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md` to describe `base_branch`/`base_pr` behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | PR implements the explicit contract agreed in issue #30, including the linked #51 need for stacked branches before broader orchestrator-to-orchestrator work. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation uses two explicit inputs, a small typed resolver, early validation, and existing workflow wiring rather than a mini-language inside `base_branch`. |

## Notes

- The highest-signal positives are the explicit input contract, early ambiguous-input failure, same-repo/open PR validation, and docs/tests covering the new behavior.
- Rubrics about preview contracts, workflow stops, merge strategy, rubric authoring, and per-file doc inventories were not scored because this PR does not materially exercise those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

<!-- sepo-agent-review-synthesis -->

> Dual-agent review by **Claude** and **Codex**.
> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #56 adds support for stacked implementation PRs.
- It introduces explicit `base_branch` and `base_pr` inputs so implementation runs can branch from another branch or from an open same-repository PR head.
- This implements the contract discussed in issue #30 and supports the broader stacked-orchestration direction referenced from #51.
- The implementation updates workflow wiring, typed helper code, tests, and relevant `.agent` docs.

## Review
The design is sound and matches the agreed contract, but one workflow regression blocks shipping. The new fetch path can fail in private repositories because it runs after checkout credentials have been disabled.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| Unauthenticated base branch fetch | BLOCKING | `.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml` now runs `git fetch origin "refs/heads/${BASE_BRANCH}"` after `actions/checkout` uses `persist-credentials: false`, so private repository fetches can fail even for default-branch implementations. |
| Missing rejection coverage for unsafe `base_pr` states | WARNING | Production code rejects closed and cross-repository `base_pr` values, but focused tests only cover happy-path PR resolution plus syntax/ambiguity validation. |
| Rubric and design checks pass | INFO | The PR keeps the input contract explicit, reuses typed helpers, updates docs, and avoids a mini-language inside `base_branch`. |

## Progress
- Existing PR discussion already confirms the intended `base_branch`/`base_pr` contract.
- Rubrics review passed with no rubric-specific required follow-up.
- I posted one inline comment for the blocking fetch issue at `.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml:195`.
- No existing inline comments duplicated that blocker before posting.

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Unauthenticated base branch fetch</summary>

**Cause:** The workflow resolves `BASE_BRANCH`, then fetches it with plain `git fetch origin "refs/heads/${BASE_BRANCH}"`. Because checkout uses `persist-credentials: false`, the remote does not retain credentials for this later fetch.

**Candidate solutions:** Fetch using the resolved GitHub token, for example with a scoped `http.extraheader`/credentialed fetch, or otherwise provide credentials only for this fetch while preserving the safer push behavior.

**Comments:** This is a regression for private repository installs and affects the default-branch case too because the new fetch always runs before branch creation.

</details>

<details>
<summary>Missing rejection coverage for unsafe `base_pr` states</summary>

**Cause:** `resolveImplementationBase()` rejects fork PRs and closed PRs, but tests do not currently lock those failure modes down.

**Candidate solutions:** Add focused tests in `.agent/src/__tests__/implementation-base.test.ts` for `isCrossRepository: true` and non-`OPEN` PR states.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the unresolved blocker and test gap both require concrete branch changes and are safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
NEEDS_REWORK

## Action Items
- [ ] Make the implementation-base fetch credentialed for private repositories while preserving safe push behavior.
- [ ] Add regression tests for closed `base_pr` rejection and cross-repository `base_pr` rejection.

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `review` -> `fix-pr` (round 4/10).

agent planner selected fix-pr: Latest review synthesis recommends FIX_PR for NEEDS_REWORK: one blocking workflow regression plus a focused test gap remain and are safe for automated changes.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777525064969 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDgwODczOTc6cmV2aWV3OjU2OmZpeF9wcjo1Njo0 -->

**Sepo pushed fixes for this PR.** Branch: `agent/implement-issue-30/codex-25147905607`. Requested by @sepo-agent.

- Fixed `.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml` so base branch fetches use the resolved GitHub token for private repos without persisting checkout credentials.
- Added focused regression coverage for rejecting cross-repository and closed `base_pr` values.
- Updated the workflow envelope assertion for the credentialed fetch path.

<details><summary>Verification</summary>

- `npm run build && node --test dist/__tests__/implementation-base.test.js dist/__tests__/envelope.test.js`

</details>

Sepo automation handoff dispatched: `fix-pr` -> `review` (round 5/10).

agent planner selected review: fix-pr succeeded after the prior NEEDS_REWORK review; another automated review is warranted to verify the credentialed fetch fix and added base_pr rejection coverage.

<!-- sepo-agent-handoff state:dispatched created:1777525227380 base64:aGFuZG9mZjpzZWxmLWV2b2x2aW5nL3JlcG86MjUxNDgyMDE2MDQ6Zml4X3ByOjU2OnJldmlldzo1Njo1 -->

## Rubrics Review

| Total Score | Verdict | Rubrics Scored |
| -- | -- | -- |
| 100 | PASS | 6 |

| Dimension | Rubric | Result | Score | Evidence |
| -- | -- | -- | -- | -- |
| coding_workflow / generic | Validate delegated route authorization | pass | 8/8 | Orchestrator and implement dispatch plumbing preserves `requested_by` while only forwarding explicit `base_branch`/`base_pr` inputs; no broader delegated route is introduced. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Make surgical changes | pass | 8/8 | The diff is scoped to stacked implementation bases: workflow inputs, dispatch forwarding, one resolver helper/CLI, focused tests, and matching docs. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Reuse existing code | pass | 8/8 | [`implementation-base.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/implementation-base.ts) reuses existing `fetchPrMeta()` and output helpers instead of embedding GitHub API parsing in workflow shell. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Keep docs in sync | pass | 7/7 | Updates [`agent-actions.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/docs/actions/agent-actions.md), [`supported-workflows.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md), and [`agent-orchestrator.md`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/docs/technical-details/agent-orchestrator.md) for stacked PR behavior. |
| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR implements the explicit #30 discussion contract: separate `base_branch` and `base_pr`, reject ambiguity, reject fork/closed base PRs, and support #51’s stacked-branch prerequisite. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | The implementation uses two typed inputs, a small resolver, early validation, and existing workflow dispatch helpers rather than overloading `base_branch` with a mini-language. |

## Notes

- The earlier private-repo fetch blocker has been addressed in the current diff: [`agent-implement.yml`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml) now fetches the resolved base branch with a token-scoped `http.extraheader` while keeping checkout credentials non-persistent.
- The focused regression gap called out earlier is also covered: [`implementation-base.test.ts`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/__tests__/implementation-base.test.ts) includes cross-repository and non-open `base_pr` rejection cases.
- Rubrics for preview contracts, workflow stops, merge strategy, rubric authoring, and per-file doc inventories were not scored because this PR does not materially exercise those behaviors.
- No rubric-specific follow-up needed.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## AI Review Synthesis

> Requested by @lolipopshock.

## Summary of PR/Issue
- PR #56 adds stacked implementation PR support for issue #30.
- It introduces explicit `base_branch` and `base_pr` inputs so implementation runs can branch from another branch or from an open same-repository PR head.
- This supports the broader stacked-orchestration direction referenced from #51 while keeping the workflow contract typed and explicit.
- The PR updates workflow wiring, typed helper code, dispatch plumbing, tests, and relevant `.agent` documentation.

## Review
The current head looks ready to ship. The prior blocking private-repo fetch issue and missing rejection coverage have both been addressed, and the independent reviews agree there are no remaining blocking or warning findings.

| Issue | Severity | Description |
| -- | -- | -- |
| No actionable issues remain | INFO | Current review artifacts report SHIP/PASS. The implementation matches the agreed `base_branch` / `base_pr` contract and includes focused regression coverage. |

## Progress
- Earlier review identified an unauthenticated base-branch fetch blocker and missing closed/fork `base_pr` rejection tests.
- The fix-pr pass updated `agent-implement.yml` to fetch with a token-scoped `http.extraheader`.

- Rubrics review passes with no rubric-specific follow-up.
- No new inline comments were posted because there are no current file/line-specific actionable findings.

## Recommended Next Step
NO_AUTOMATED_ACTION: no unresolved actionable work remains for an automated fix-pr pass.

## Final Verdict
SHIP

## Action Items
- [ ] No required branch-change action items remain.

If that base branch not set then it's by default main right? Which line of code?
@sepo-agent /answer

Yes, but more precisely: it defaults to the repo’s default branch, which is currently `main`.

The fallback is here:

- [`implementation-base.ts` lines 89-91](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/implementation-base.ts#L89-L91)

```ts
return {
  baseBranch: defaultBranch,
  source: "default_branch",
};
```

That `defaultBranch` comes from the workflow here:

- [`agent-implement.yml` line 176](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.github/workflows/agent-implement.yml#L176)

```yaml
DEFAULT_BRANCH: ${{ github.event.repository.default_branch }}
```

So if neither `base_branch` nor `base_pr` is set, it uses `github.event.repository.default_branch`, not a hardcoded `main`.
@sepo-agent /answer check my last code review comment about delim

That `delim` is the heredoc delimiter used when writing a value into GitHub Actions’ `$GITHUB_ENV`.

In this line:

[`implementation-base.ts#L100`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/implementation-base.ts#L100)

```ts
const delim = `DELIM_${randomBytes(8).toString("hex")}`;
```

it creates a random marker like `DELIM_a1b2c3d4...`, then this line writes:

[`implementation-base.ts#L101`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/implementation-base.ts#L101)

```ts
appendFileSync(envFile, `${name}<<${delim}\n${value}\n${delim}\n`);
```

That is GitHub Actions syntax for setting env vars safely, especially if the value could contain newlines:

```text
BASE_BRANCH<<DELIM_xxx
some-value
DELIM_xxx
```

Here it sets `BASE_BRANCH` for later workflow steps. The same pattern already exists for outputs in [`output.ts#L17-L18`](https://github.com/self-evolving/repo/blob/99cbb27c6308d7ff2c8b300f18b06a937bf93c1e/.agent/src/output.ts#L17-L18). For branch names specifically, values are validated as single-line, so this is conservative but consistent.
@sepo-agent /orchestrate firstly fix merge conflicts then launch a review agent

| coding_workflow / generic | Read docs and linked context first | pass | 6/6 | The PR implements the explicit issue #30 contract and supports the stacked-branch prerequisite discussed from issue #51. |
| coding_style / generic | Prefer simple implementations | pass | 7/7 | Uses two structured inputs, early validation, and a small typed resolver rather than overloading `base_branch` with a mini-language. |
| coding_workflow / generic | No per-file inventory tables in docs | pass | 6/6 | The docs describe workflow behavior and structural rules; they do not add stale per-file inventory tables. |

## Notes

- The current diff addresses the earlier review’s private-repo fetch concern by using a token-scoped `http.extraheader` for the base branch fetch while keeping checkout credentials non-persistent.
- Regression coverage now includes default branch fallback, explicit branch, same-repo open `base_pr`, cross-repo rejection, closed PR rejection, ambiguous inputs, and orchestration forwarding.
- Rubrics for preview contracts, workflow stops, merge strategy, stateful failures, rubric authoring, and follow-up one-shot defaults were not scored because this PR does not materially change those behaviors.

## Findings

- **INFO:** No rubric-specific findings.

## Final Rubric Verdict

PASS

## Issue Details

<details>
<summary>Merge conflict in supported workflows docs</summary>

**Cause:** Current `main` changed the review cleanup documentation to cover both review synthesis and rubrics review cleanup, while this PR adds stacked implementation documentation in the same nearby section.

**Candidate solutions:** Rebase or merge `main`, then resolve `.agent/docs/architecture/supported-workflows.md` by keeping the `base_branch` / `base_pr` stacked implementation paragraph and the current `main` wording for `AGENT_COLLAPSE_OLD_REVIEWS`.

**Comments:** This is a mergeability blocker, not a design disagreement. The code-level issues from the previous review appear fixed.

</details>

## Recommended Next Step
FIX_PR: the unresolved merge conflict requires a concrete branch change and is safe for an automated fix-pr pass.

NEEDS_REWORK

title: Support stacked implementation PRs
